summary_strategy: Reframe summary around building and operating production SIEM solutions with emphasis on reliability, documentation, and measurable risk reduction—aligning with Coalfire's mission-driven tone
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format to group skills into high-impact categories, prioritizing Cloud & DevSecOps, Security Data & AI, and Security Engineering & Automation; remove duplicate entries (e.g., 'Security Tooling' listed twice), avoid empty phrases, and ensure every bullet maps to JD requirements

experience_focus.must_highlight: production SIEM-equivalent pipelines, log ingestion and normalization at scale, rule tuning and alert reduction, runbook-driven automation
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 7000+ endpoints, 10000+ devices, 2900-user identity portal, 500 hours saved annually, 40%+ false positive reduction
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: invented tools (e.g., 'Coraline'), speculative ownership ('pioneered', 'established the technical standard'), unverified percentages beyond what source explicitly states

risk_controls: Replace all invented tool names with generic functional descriptions (e.g., 'security correlation platform' instead of 'Coraline')

Remove all speculative impact claims unless directly supported by source (e.g., '40%+ false positive reduction' only if explicitly stated in original resume—currently it is not, so remove or rephrase as 'reduced alert volume through baseline analysis' without percentage)

Avoid terms like 'pioneered', 'established the technical standard', or 'operationalized standardized asset remediation' unless directly quoted from source; replace with neutral language like 'developed and deployed'
Do not invent metrics for Simple.biz or PhishFinder unless present in baseline; cap at 'improved compliance rates' without specific % unless cited
